purpose: A horizontal flexbox layout primitive. Arranges children in a horizontal row with configurable justification, alignment, and spacing. The horizontal-only sibling of VBox. Rendered by the plugin as a pass-through frame using Figma Auto Layout (HORIZONTAL).
whenToUse: Group child components in a horizontal row inside a page or panel
Compose custom action bars or button clusters
Align related items side-by-side (icon + text, avatar + name)
As a lightweight alternative to Toolbar when semantics don't matter
whenNotToUse: For a semantic action bar — use Toolbar or Bar
For a top-of-page shell — use ShellBar
When children need to wrap responsively — use FlexBox with wrap
As a page-level layout — use DynamicPage or Page
doRules: Set consistent itemSpacing across the row
Use justifyContent for alignment (Start, End, Center, SpaceBetween)
Set width='100%' when the box should stretch to parent
Prefer HBox over inline styles when the layout is reusable
dontRules: Do not use HBox for form field layout — use Form / SimpleForm
Do not nest deeply — becomes hard to reason about
Do not use for a full-width toolbar — use Toolbar/Bar
Do not mix with absolute-positioned children

layoutGuidance.placement: Inside pages, panels, cards, custom composite components
layoutGuidance.sizing: Width hugs content or fills parent; height hugs tallest child
layoutGuidance.alignment: justifyContent + alignItems props control child positioning

contentGuidance.contentRules: Children are any renderable components; not semantic

responsiveBehavior.M: Full row; may wrap if items overflow

requirements: role='group' when children share semantic purpose; otherwise none
Focus order follows left-to-right visual order
Children provide their own accessible names
No aria-label on HBox itself (it's a layout, not a landmark)
